title: fimbristylis complanata subsp. keniaeensis
intro: Fimbristylis complanata subsp. keniaeensis, also known as Kenyan Fimbri, is a type of sedge plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It is a perennial herb that grows in wetland areas, and has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, and promoting wound healing. Fimbristylis complanata subsp. keniaeensis can be prepared into herbal remedies such as infusions, decoctions, and poultices, and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries.
